message:
fix(widgets): generalize clear_text_row → clear_text_area and apply across the entire widget set

Re-renders that produce shorter content than the previous frame were
leaving stale glyphs behind whenever a widget did not own a Block or
explicit background fill. Tests on Badge / Align / and friends caught
the regression: render "LONG", then re-render "OK", and the trailing
"NG" stayed on screen.

## crates/ftui-widgets/src/lib.rs

Promote the existing single-row helper `clear_text_row` to a full-area
helper `clear_text_area`, then re-implement `clear_text_row` as a
single-row call to it. The helper still returns early on zero
width/height, builds one styled space cell, and uses
`Frame::buffer.fill` so the rect is wiped in one pass. Internal
visibility (`pub(crate)`) — this is not part of the public widget API.

## Widgets adopting clear_text_area / clear_text_row

Every widget that owned an area but did not previously erase it before
rendering now calls the appropriate helper before drawing:

  align, badge, block, columns, command_palette/mod, decision_card,
  drift_visualization, emoji, error_boundary, file_picker,
  focus/manager, help, history_panel, input, json_view, layout,
  layout_debugger, list, log_viewer, notification_queue, padding,
  paginator, panel, paragraph, popover, pretty, progress, rule,
  scrollbar, sparkline, spinner, status_line, stopwatch, table, tabs,
  textarea, timer, toast, tree, validation_error, virtualized,
  voi_debug_overlay.

Some widgets (notably `align`) needed a per-cell clear that resets
`CellContent` to `EMPTY` while preserving styling already applied to
the buffer by the parent — those use a direct `cell.content =
CellContent::EMPTY` loop instead of the row helper. Either way the
intent is the same: a widget that owns a wrapper rect must wipe stale
child glyphs before rendering its current child.

## Test coverage for the regression

The following new unit tests fail without the corresponding clear:

  - badge::render_shorter_label_clears_stale_suffix
  - align::smaller_second_render_clears_old_child_region
  - align::zero_size_child_clears_previous_content
  - align::stateful_smaller_second_render_clears_old_child_region

(Plus analogous "render twice, second is smaller, assert no leftover
glyphs" tests added to many of the other widgets touched above.)

These collectively pin the contract: re-rendering a widget at the
same area with shorter content must produce a frame that contains
no glyphs from the previous render.
